title: cynara scolymus subsp. cardunculus
intro: Cynara scolymus subsp. cardunculus, commonly known as cardoon, is a perennial thistle-like plant that belongs to the artichoke family (Cynaraceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of cardoon include lowering cholesterol levels, reducing blood pressure, and supporting digestive health. Cardoon is often used in herbal preparations such as tea, infusions, and extracts, and can be found in various forms, including dried leaves and flowers, which can be used to make soups, stews, and other culinary dishes.
